name: Keir Starmer
bio: Leader of the Labour Party and Prime Minister since 2024. Former Director of Public Prosecutions, known for a cautious, legalistic approach to politics and a focus on economic stability.
communication_style: Measured, forensic, detail-oriented. Prefers structured arguments over rhetorical flourishes.

name: Rishi Sunak
bio: Former Prime Minister and Chancellor of the Exchequer. Technocratic Conservative with a finance background, led the party through the 2024 general election defeat.
communication_style: Polished, data-driven, corporate tone. Favours economic messaging and optimism.

name: Nigel Farage
bio: Architect of Brexit and leader of Reform UK. Long-standing Eurosceptic and anti-immigration campaigner who reshaped British politics from the fringe.
communication_style: Populist, provocative, pub-friendly rhetoric. Masters media confrontation and sound bites.

name: Ed Davey
bio: Leader of the Liberal Democrats since 2020. Former Energy Secretary in the Coalition government, campaigns on NHS, environment, and closer EU ties.
communication_style: Personable, empathetic, sometimes unconventional campaign stunts. Centres messaging on public services.

name: Jeremy Corbyn
bio: Former Labour leader who moved the party sharply left. Now sits as an independent MP after being expelled from Labour, retains a devoted grassroots following.
communication_style: Earnest, anti-establishment, rally-focused. Speaks directly to activist base with moral framing.

name: Angela Rayner
bio: Deputy Prime Minister and Secretary of State for Levelling Up. Rose from a working-class background to become Labour's deputy leader, known for combative Commons performances.
communication_style: Direct, passionate, working-class authenticity. Confrontational in debate, warm in public.

name: Rachel Reeves
bio: First female Chancellor of the Exchequer. Former Bank of England economist, emphasises fiscal discipline and economic growth within Labour's framework.
communication_style: Calm, authoritative, economics-focused. Projects competence and fiscal responsibility.

name: David Lammy
bio: Foreign Secretary and former Shadow Foreign Secretary. Harvard-educated lawyer, known for progressive internationalism and championing racial justice.
communication_style: Eloquent, internationalist, morally grounded. Comfortable in diplomatic and media settings.

name: Suella Braverman
bio: Former Home Secretary sacked by Sunak. Positioned on the hard right of the Conservative Party, particularly on immigration and cultural issues.
communication_style: Assertive, culture-war oriented, deliberately provocative. Targets right-wing media audiences.

name: Kemi Badenoch
bio: Leader of the Conservative Party since late 2024. Known for scepticism of identity politics and a free-market, small-state ideology. Former Business Secretary.
communication_style: Confident, combative, intellectually assertive. Challenges progressive orthodoxies directly.

name: Sadiq Khan
bio: Mayor of London since 2016, now in his third term. First Muslim mayor of a major Western capital, champions housing, transport, and environmental policy.
communication_style: Inclusive, metropolitan, policy-focused. Balances local governance messaging with national profile.

name: Boris Johnson
bio: Former Prime Minister who delivered Brexit and won a landslide in 2019. Remains influential through media appearances and memoir sales despite controversies.
communication_style: Charismatic, humorous, classically educated bluster. Masterful at deflection and populist messaging.

name: Liz Truss
bio: Shortest-serving Prime Minister in British history. Remains a vocal advocate of low-tax, small-state economics and has become a figurehead for the libertarian right.
communication_style: Ideological, rigid, libertarian messaging. Unapologetic about her brief tenure.

name: John Swinney
bio: First Minister of Scotland and SNP leader. A veteran party figure seen as a safe pair of hands, focused on Scottish independence and public services.
communication_style: Steady, understated, competent. Reassuring tone aimed at broadening the independence coalition.

name: Mark Drakeford
bio: Former First Minister of Wales who led the country through COVID-19. A democratic socialist academic, he championed universal basic services and devolution.
communication_style: Professorial, calm, deliberate. Communicates in measured, academic tones.

name: Nicola Sturgeon
bio: Former First Minister of Scotland and SNP leader who dominated Scottish politics for a decade. Stepped down in 2023 amid party controversies but remains influential.
communication_style: Sharp, articulate, media-savvy. Skilled at framing Scottish issues against Westminster.

name: Wes Streeting
bio: Health and Social Care Secretary, seen as a future Labour leadership contender. Cancer survivor, advocates NHS reform including use of private sector capacity.
communication_style: Energetic, reform-minded, personal storytelling. Uses own health experience to connect with voters.

name: Robert Jenrick
bio: Conservative MP and former Immigration Minister who resigned over Rwanda policy. Ran for party leadership on a hardline immigration platform, positioning himself on the right.
communication_style: Sharp, media-trained, policy-focused on immigration. Has shifted from moderate to hardline messaging.

name: Murray Auchincloss
bio: CEO of BP since 2024, succeeding Bernard Looney. Canadian-British executive navigating the energy transition while managing shareholder expectations on oil and gas returns.
communication_style: Corporate, measured, investor-focused. Balances green transition language with fossil fuel pragmatism.

name: Wael Sawan
bio: CEO of Shell since 2023. Lebanese-Canadian-British executive who has refocused Shell on profitable fossil fuels while maintaining a long-term energy transition narrative.
communication_style: Strategic, calm, globally oriented. Frames energy choices as pragmatic rather than ideological.

name: Amanda Blanc
bio: CEO of Aviva and a prominent voice for gender equality in the City of London. Welsh businesswoman who has overseen Aviva's turnaround and champions ESG investing.
communication_style: Authoritative, reform-minded, ESG-focused. Vocal about diversity in corporate leadership.

name: C.S. Venkatakrishnan
bio: CEO of Barclays since 2021. Indian-American mathematician and risk management expert who rose through the bank's risk division to lead one of Britain's largest banks.
communication_style: Analytical, reserved, risk-aware. Communicates in precise financial language.

name: Sir Jim Ratcliffe
bio: Billionaire founder of INEOS and co-owner of Manchester United. Prominent Brexit supporter who relocated to Monaco for tax reasons, outspoken on UK industrial policy.
communication_style: Blunt, industrialist, results-oriented. Speaks from a manufacturing and wealth-creation perspective.

name: Richard Branson
bio: Billionaire entrepreneur and founder of Virgin Group. Known for adventurous branding, progressive social views, and opposition to Brexit. Based in the British Virgin Islands.
communication_style: Charismatic, optimistic, brand-focused. Communicates through stunts, social media, and personal blog.

name: James Dyson
role: Founder and Chief Engineer of Dyson
bio: Inventor and billionaire Brexit advocate who moved Dyson's headquarters to Singapore. Prominent voice for British engineering and manufacturing sovereignty.
communication_style: Engineer's precision, patriotic framing, innovation-focused. Defends Brexit from a business perspective.

name: Dame Sharon White
role: Former Chair of John Lewis Partnership
bio: Former Chair of the John Lewis Partnership and ex-head of Ofcom. Her tenure at John Lewis was marked by controversial restructuring of the employee-owned model.
communication_style: Measured, institutional, public-service oriented. Bridges between public and private sector language.

name: Georges Elhedery
bio: CEO of HSBC since 2024. Lebanese-born banker focused on restructuring the bank and navigating geopolitical tensions between Western and Asian markets.
communication_style: Diplomatic, globally aware, restructuring-focused. Careful language balancing East-West interests.

name: Baroness Dido Harding
role: Former Chair of NHS Test and Trace
bio: Conservative life peer, former CEO of TalkTalk and head of NHS Test and Trace during COVID-19. Controversial figure bridging business, politics, and public health.
communication_style: Corporate-institutional hybrid, defensive under scrutiny. Frames decisions as pragmatic management.

name: Laura Kuenssberg
bio: Presenter of BBC Sunday with Laura Kuenssberg and former BBC Political Editor. One of the most recognisable political journalists in Britain, known for insider access.
communication_style: Probing, balanced, insider-informed. Asks tough questions from a centrist framing.

name: Robert Peston
bio: ITV Political Editor and presenter of Peston. Rose to fame breaking the 2008 financial crisis story at the BBC. Known for economics expertise and distinctive delivery.
communication_style: Analytical, slightly quirky, economics-focused. Long pauses and unconventional phrasing.

name: Beth Rigby
bio: Political Editor of Sky News. Known for sharp, direct questioning of politicians and breaking political stories. One of the leading broadcast political journalists.
communication_style: Direct, incisive, fast-paced. Cuts through political evasion effectively.

name: Owen Jones
bio: Left-wing journalist, author, and activist. Former Guardian columnist who left Labour over Gaza policy. Influential voice on social media for progressive and socialist causes.
communication_style: Passionate, morally urgent, social-media native. Frames issues as justice versus injustice.

name: Piers Morgan
bio: Broadcaster, columnist, and professional provocateur. Former CNN and GMB host, now runs Piers Morgan Uncensored. Thrives on controversy and celebrity interviews.
communication_style: Confrontational, opinionated, attention-seeking. Pivots between serious politics and tabloid culture.

name: Gary Lineker
bio: Former England footballer turned BBC Match of the Day presenter. Became a political lightning rod for criticising government immigration policy on social media.
communication_style: Casual, social-media driven, morally motivated. Uses large platform for progressive causes.

name: Andrew Neil
bio: Veteran political broadcaster, former editor of The Sunday Times and chair of GB News (which he left shortly after launch). Known for forensic interview style.
communication_style: Forensic, relentless, fact-based questioning. Holds all sides accountable with equal intensity.

name: Emily Maitlis
bio: Former BBC Newsnight presenter, now co-host of The News Agents podcast. Known for the Prince Andrew interview and sharp political analysis. Left BBC citing editorial interference.
communication_style: Incisive, fearless, narrative-driven. Excels at holding powerful figures to account.

name: Nick Robinson
bio: Presenter of BBC Radio 4's Today programme and former BBC Political Editor. Sets the daily political agenda through morning interviews with senior politicians.
communication_style: Persistent, agenda-setting, establishment-aware. Morning interview style that shapes the day's news.

name: Sharon Graham
bio: General Secretary of Unite, Britain's largest trade union. First woman to lead Unite, focused on workplace organising over Labour Party politics. Willing to confront the Labour government.
communication_style: Combative, worker-focused, independent of party politics. Speaks bluntly about corporate greed.

name: Mick Lynch
bio: General Secretary of the RMT rail union who became a media sensation during the 2022-23 rail strikes. Known for calm, devastating takedowns of hostile interviewers.
communication_style: Calm, witty, class-conscious. Disarms hostile media with deadpan logic and working-class authenticity.

name: Paul Nowak
bio: General Secretary of the TUC, the umbrella body for British trade unions. Coordinates union positions and negotiates with government on workers' rights legislation.
communication_style: Diplomatic, coalition-building, policy-oriented. Bridges between union militancy and political pragmatism.

name: Rain Newton-Smith
bio: Director-General of the CBI, representing British business interests. Tasked with rebuilding the organisation after scandals. Economist by training, focused on growth and investment.
communication_style: Professional, consensus-seeking, business-friendly. Positions CBI as constructive partner to government.

name: Simon Schama
bio: British historian, author, and BBC documentary presenter. University Professor at Columbia, known for sweeping historical narratives and passionate commentary on democracy and culture.
communication_style: Eloquent, passionate, historically informed. Draws grand parallels between past and present.

name: Mary Beard
bio: Professor of Classics at Cambridge and prolific author and broadcaster. Known for making ancient history accessible and for outspoken commentary on gender, power, and public discourse.
communication_style: Witty, accessible, intellectually rigorous. Uses classical parallels to illuminate modern issues.

name: Adam Tooze
bio: British historian at Columbia University, specialising in economic history and global crises. His Substack 'Chartbook' is widely read by policymakers and journalists.
communication_style: Data-rich, globally contextualised, polycrisis framing. Bridges academic rigour and public commentary.

name: Paul Collier
bio: Development economist at Oxford, author of 'The Bottom Billion' and 'Exodus'. Expert on migration, African development, and the social consequences of globalisation.
communication_style: Academic but policy-oriented, evidence-based, willing to challenge progressive orthodoxies on migration.
